name: AI Model Eval Lab
description: Proposal template for reviewing AI models and agent systems, comparing strengths and weaknesses, and producing grounded recommendations.
author: ClawMax Team

communities.name: AI Model Eval
communities.description: Shared coordination space for model comparisons, benchmarks, and evaluation notes.

groups.name: Benchmarks
groups.description: Evaluation criteria, tests, and result notes.

groups.name: Comparisons
groups.description: Direct tradeoffs across models, agents, and providers.

groups.name: Recommendations
groups.description: Decision notes, fit by use case, and next experiments.

groups.name: Status
groups.description: Current scope, blockers, and final recommendation state.

agents.name: Eval Lead
agents.role: Evaluation lead — defines the test bar, comparison frame, and final recommendation.

agents.name: Eval Runner
agents.role: Testing specialist — runs comparisons, captures outputs, and documents failure modes.

agents.name: Benchmark Designer
agents.role: Benchmark specialist — defines the scenarios, rubrics, and success criteria.

agents.name: Recommendation Editor
agents.role: Recommendation specialist — turns raw comparison notes into a decision-ready summary.

workflows.name: Model Eval Kickoff
workflows.content:
# Model Eval Kickoff

You are the Eval Lead.

## Project Configuration
> **Customize these before applying:**

- **Models or agents to compare:** [list]
- **Primary use case:** [coding, research, support, automation, custom]
- **Evaluation focus:** [quality, speed, cost, safety, reliability, custom]
- **Test scenarios:** [few-shot tasks, benchmark set, workflow trial, custom]
- **Output artifact:** [comparison memo, eval report, recommendation]

## Your Tasks
1. Restate the comparison set and use case
2. Ask Benchmark Designer for the rubric and scenarios
3. Ask Eval Runners for structured results
4. Ask Recommendation Editor for the decision memo
5. Post the eval plan to Status

workflows.name: Benchmark Design Review
workflows.description: Define the scenarios, rubric, and success criteria for the evaluation.
workflows.content:
# Benchmark Design Review

1. Review the models, use case, and evaluation focus defined in kickoff
2. Turn them into a concrete rubric, scenario set, and scoring approach
3. Make sure the benchmark covers the most important tradeoffs without ballooning scope
4. Call out what the benchmark can measure well versus what will remain subjective
5. Post the benchmark design review to Benchmarks and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Structured Eval Run
workflows.description: Run the agreed scenarios and capture the strongest results and failure modes.
workflows.content:
# Structured Eval Run

1. Use the approved benchmark design to compare the models or agents in scope
2. Capture the strongest wins, losses, quality differences, speed tradeoffs, and notable failure modes
3. Keep the results structured enough that a recommendation can be justified clearly
4. Distinguish direct eval evidence from interpretation or guesswork
5. Post the structured eval run results to Comparisons and a short status note to Status

workflows.name: Comparison Memo
workflows.content:
# Comparison Memo

1. Compare the models across the agreed dimensions
2. Name the clear winner, best budget option, and biggest caution
3. Tie the recommendation back to the target use case
4. Post the final memo to Recommendations and the short summary to Status
